findings.evidence: `Rectangle.lean:202-203` defines `GridRectangle.AvoidsMarkings` using `G.OSet ∪ G.XSet`; `Rectangle.lean:605-609` exposes the oriented version; `Rectangle.lean:745-748` already has the analogous general `avoidsMarkings_transpose`, while `BlockedRectangle.lean:136-140` reproves the marking-swap fact only inside fully blocked rectangles.
findings.fix: Add `GridRectangle.avoidsMarkings_swapMarkings : R.AvoidsMarkings G.swapMarkings ↔ R.AvoidsMarkings G` and/or `GridRectangleBetween.avoidsMarkings_swapMarkings`, then derive `fullyBlockedRectangles_swapMarkings` from it.
findings.issue: `fullyBlockedRectangles_swapMarkings` proves the `O`/`X` swap symmetry by opening the fully blocked set, but the same argument is the more general rectangle-level fact that marking avoidance is invariant under `G.swapMarkings`.

summary: The transpose API is now at the natural equivalence level, but the marking-swap symmetry is still proved only after specializing to fully blocked rectangle sets. The natural statement belongs at the rectangle marking-avoidance level and should feed the set/count/differential corollaries.
